title: T-044 — Sync queue flusher (online-event-driven replay with idempotent server dedup)
prompt:
# T-044 — Sync queue flusher (online-event-driven replay with idempotent server dedup)

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5 (IndexedDB sync queue), §10 (Idempotent mutations [R14])
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Flusher that listens for window 'online' event, drains the IndexedDB queue from T-043 in FIFO order, and POSTs each mutation to its endpoint with the X-Transaction-Id header. The receiving routes use the idempotency helper from T-024 to dedup replays.

## Implementation contract
(1) Add packages/sync-queue/src/flusher.ts exporting startFlusher() that subscribes to window.addEventListener('online', flushOnce) + a 30-second interval fallback. (2) flushOnce() reads listPending() from T-043, sends each in order via fetch(endpoint, { method, body, headers: { 'X-Transaction-Id': transaction_id, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }). (3) On 2xx or 409 (duplicate per T-024 idempotency) → remove(transaction_id). (4) On 5xx or network error → leave in queue + exponential backoff (5min/30min/2h capped at 12h). (5) Export stopFlusher() for unmount. (6) Vitest covers happy path, 409 dedup-success path, 5xx retry path with mocked fetch.

## Files (scope)
- packages/sync-queue/src/flusher.ts [create]
- packages/sync-queue/src/__tests__/flusher.test.ts [create]
- packages/sync-queue/src/index.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given 3 mutations queued and the network goes online, when window dispatches 'online', then flushOnce sends 3 fetches in FIFO order each carrying X-Transaction-Id matching the queued transaction_id
2. Given a fetch returns HTTP 409 (server already saw this transaction_id per T-024), when flushOnce processes it, then the entry is removed from the queue (treat dedup-success as success)
3. Given a fetch returns HTTP 503, when flushOnce processes it, then the entry stays in the queue and the backoff timer is set to the next attempt (≥5 minutes)
4. Given startFlusher() is called twice, when the second call returns, then only one online listener is registered (idempotent start — no double flushing)

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/sync-queue/src/__tests__/flusher.test.ts with mocked fetch + a fake online event before flusher.ts exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/sync-queue test'

## Risk red lines
- Do not flush in parallel — FIFO order is the §10 R14 contract
- Do not delete an entry on 4xx other than 409 (caller error needs human intervention)
- Do not exceed the 12-hour backoff cap — beyond that, surface to a UI alert (deferred — separate task)

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-024
- T-043

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.
